---
title: "About"
subtitle: "Austin P. Morrissey — AI safety researcher, biosecurity evals, Pivotal Fellow"
description: "Austin P. Morrissey is an AI safety researcher working on biosecurity capability evaluations as a Pivotal Research Fellow mentored by SecureBio."
date-modified: today
toc: false
image: images/austin-p-morrissey-headshot.jpg
---

<img src="images/austin-p-morrissey-headshot.jpg" alt="Austin P. Morrissey, rendered as a portrait composed of typographic symbols" class="about-portrait" />

## Who I am

I'm **Austin P. Morrissey** — also Austin Morrissey, also [Austin Patrick on Substack](https://austinpatrick.substack.com) (the P stands for Patrick). They're all me. I'm an AI safety researcher focused on biosecurity capability evaluations — designing experiments that measure whether large language models could provide uplift to actors seeking to misuse biology, and how robustly current model safeguards hold up.

I'm currently a [Pivotal Research](https://www.pivotal-research.org/) Fellow, mentored by Jasper at [SecureBio](https://securebio.org/).

## What I work on

My research sits at the intersection of three areas:

1. **AI capability evaluations.** Building model-agnostic eval suites in [Inspect AI](https://inspect.ai-safety-institute.org.uk/) that distinguish "the model won't say" from "the model can't say." Most current safety evals conflate these.

2. **Biosecurity threat modeling.** Working through which technical bottlenecks actually matter for an actor with malicious intent, and which the current generation of LLMs meaningfully changes.

3. **Adversarial elicitation methodology.** Multi-turn jailbreak sequences, classifier characterization, and fine-grained mapping of where model safety boundaries actually are versus where they're advertised.

This work is **defensive dual-use** — designing the measurement, not the misuse. It's the kind of work that exists precisely so that policy and labs can act on grounded evidence rather than speculation.

## Background

- **Pivotal Research Fellow** (2025–present) — AI safety / biosecurity
- **MSc Medical Science** — Boston University (2023)
- **BSc Biochemistry** — University of Massachusetts Amherst (2020)
- Wet-lab R&D experience in mRNA science

## Find me

| | |
|---|---|
| Writing | [Substack: Rational Biology](https://austinpatrick.substack.com) |
| Code | [GitHub: gvmfhy](https://github.com/gvmfhy) |
| CV / professional | [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/austin-p-morrissey/) |
| Academic | [Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=uMwJNXEAAAAJ&hl=en) · [ORCID 0009-0007-8578-2687](https://orcid.org/0009-0007-8578-2687) |
| Email | austinpatrickmorrissey \[at\] gmail \[dot\] com |

## Press / collaboration

If you're a journalist, podcast host, or researcher who'd like to talk about AI biosecurity evals, reach out by email above. I'm particularly happy to discuss methodology, eval design, and what current results do and don't tell us about model risk.

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
**Note on this site.** Built with [Quarto](https://quarto.org). Source on [GitHub](https://github.com/gvmfhy). Essays cross-post to my [Substack](https://austinpatrick.substack.com); the canonical version of each lives here.
:::
